INSERT INTO sample_data VALUES ('hello world'), ('duckdb is awesome'), ('foo bar baz'), ('hello duckdb');
SELECT regexp_replace(text_column, 'duck(.*)', 'quack') AS modified_column FROM sample_data;
CREATE TABLE second_data (id INT, desc_column VARCHAR);
INSERT INTO second_data VALUES (1, 'This is a test'), (2, 'Regular expressions are powerful'), (3, 'Hello world');
SELECT a.text_column, b.desc_column, regexp_replace(a.text_column, '[aeiou]', '*') AS masked_text FROM sample_data a JOIN second_data b ON a.text_column LIKE '%hello%';
SELECT text_column, regexp_replace(text_column, '^h.llo$', 'hi there', 'i') FROM sample_data WHERE text_column LIKE 'h%';
SELECT regexp_replace(CONCAT(text_column, ' ', desc_column), '[ ]+', ' ') AS cleaned_text FROM sample_data CROSS JOIN second_data;
SELECT id, regexp_replace(text_column, '([a-z]+)', UPPER('$1')) FROM sample_data WHERE REGEXP_LIKE(text_column, 'hello');
SELECT id, regexp_replace(text_column, '(duck)', '\U\1\E') FROM sample_data WHERE text_column LIKE '%duck%';
